source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/DK_제품별 자료 정리(pdf)/2. HAD1417MCW/5. Non-clinical considerations/002_EN 62220-1-1 Test report - HAD1417MCW.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: EN 62220-1-1 test report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector covering physical, operational, functional, exposure, safety, and test-result characteristics.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports non-clinical characterization of a solid-state X-ray imaging detector, including MTF and DQE measurements under EN 62220-1-1.
keyPoints: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, including MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
The device is not for fluoroscopic use and is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
The active area is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm with 2466 x 3040 active area pixels.
The scintillator is CsI and converts x-ray energy to visible light.
Measured ADD DQE values are 49.5% at 0.1 lp/mm, 43.8% at 0.5 lp/mm, 39.7% at 1 lp/mm, 31.6% at 2 lp/mm, and 21.5% at 3 lp/mm.

concepts.evidence: The detective quantum efficiency (DQE) was evaluated as a function of spatial frequency over a wide range of incident exposure levels.

evidence.claim: The document is EN 62220-1-1 Test Report Doc. No. A-TCF-5-058, Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report Doc. No. A-TCF-5-058 Rev. No. 0

evidence.claim: The report is for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report For ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The report revision date is Aug/5th/2020.
The stated standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for determination of detective quantum efficiency of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: The active pixel area is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm

evidence.claim: The number of active area pixels is 2466 x 3040 = 7,496,640.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of active area pixel 2466 x 3040 = 7,496,640

evidence.claim: The detector uses a CsI scintillator to convert X-ray to visible light to charge the photodiode.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator • CsI • Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode

evidence.claim: The device frame rate is 1 fps.
All image data are transmitted and stored as 14-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 14-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two-dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: The device is not for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not for fluoroscopic.

evidence.claim: Measured ADD DQE values are 49.5%, 43.8%, 39.7%, 31.6%, and 21.5% at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp respectively.
Measured ADD MTF values are 96.7%, 83.6%, 63.2%, 30.9%, and 14.7% at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp respectively.
The device does not provide under-scanning functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: This device does not provide the applicable functionality.

evidence.claim: The active area is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The report references 21CFR1020.31 or .32 for x-ray beam alignment and collimation requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Describe how x-ray beam alignment and collimation to the “active” area of the device will be achieved to meet the requirements of 21CFR1020.31 or .32.

evidence.claim: Readout after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds, and image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds at which time the detector is ready for the next exposure. Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: The ADD meets regulatory standards as outlined in ADD safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the ADD safety report: 397950, EMC report: NK-20-E-0336, NK-20-E-0337

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text does not explicitly name FDA; authority is inferred only from 21CFR1020.31 or .32 references.
Source path indicates HAD1417MCW, but the document text identifies the device as ADD Abyz Digital Detector; product model relationship may require confirmation.
Page numbering is inconsistent: first page states Page 1/29 while following pages state Page 2/26 through Page 26/26.
Several sections contain placeholder headings or missing plotted image details where figures are represented only as LineChart or blank extracted text.
